title: henckelia nervosa
intro: Henckelia nervosa, also known as the Indian pipe cactus or Asiatic toothache plant, is a species of flowering plant native to the eastern Himalayas and southern China. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenoid compounds. The health benefits of Henckelia nervosa include its ability to relieve toothache pain, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of a tincture, decoction, or powder, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
